description: Animation is derived from truth-after-hash replay data, not hand-authored keyframes. PresentationBricks consumes action labels, phase events, contact events, and capability deltas to produce clip IDs, additive reactions, and a retargeting bridge mapping 16 truth joints to 20 presentation bones (including grip_r and grip_l).

clip_library.additive: cut_arc, thrust_line, shield_or_body_bash, hook_bind_strain, grappling_reach, push_extension, leg_attack_extension

presentation_brick_primitives.name: locomotion
presentation_brick_primitives.consumes: truth poses, step/pivot/recover action labels, capability balance state

presentation_brick_primitives.name: guard_transition
presentation_brick_primitives.consumes: guard/parry/brace action labels and facing direction

presentation_brick_primitives.name: weapon_handling
presentation_brick_primitives.consumes: weapon id, action labels, grip capability, truth joint mapping

presentation_brick_primitives.name: bind_hook
presentation_brick_primitives.consumes: contact event material result and hook/bind action labels

presentation_brick_primitives.name: stumble
presentation_brick_primitives.consumes: balance capability delta and contact events

presentation_brick_primitives.name: collapse
presentation_brick_primitives.consumes: severe balance loss or injury events

presentation_brick_primitives.name: fight_film_camera
presentation_brick_primitives.consumes: contact events, turn transitions, replay timeline

notes: PresentationBricks artifacts are generated per-replay by the truth engine binary
The renderer reads the sequence JSON to select clip IDs per frame
Procedural pose offsets are applied in the vertex shader based on clip ID
No NVIDIA MotionBricks SDK is claimed; this is an internal MotionBricks-inspired API
